summary: Kenneth Suna demonstrates three methods to profit from investments without selling: dividend collection, selling covered calls on long-term holdings, and collateralized lending (pledged asset lines). The primary focus is on covered calls as an income strategy, using Palantir as an example where weekly call sales generate $370+ per contract.
takeaways: Sell weekly expiring covered calls to generate consistent premium income without liquidating shares
Require 100 shares minimum per covered call contract to establish position-building as long-term strategy
Use delta as probability metric: 30% delta ≈ 30% probability of assignment (e.g., $99 strike on $93 stock)
Target out-of-the-money strikes (e.g., $94-$99 on $93 stock) to reduce assignment risk while collecting premium
Can buy back options before expiration to lock in gains and retain shares if underlying rallies
Weekly expirations provide more frequent premium collection opportunities than longer-dated contracts
Pledged asset lines (Charles Schwab) allow borrowing against portfolio without liquidation, avoiding capital gains taxes

strategy_candidates.change: Implement tiered strike selection rule: sell 70% of weekly calls at 30-delta OTM strikes for higher probability of keeping shares, and 30% at 50+ delta strikes for higher premium capture
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth demonstrates both conservative ($99, 30-delta) and aggressive ($94, higher probability) strike selection on Palantir, noting the trade-off between premium size and assignment risk; Brian's $50k Premium Desk could systematize this mix to optimize $1,250/mo target

title: Can you MAKE MONEY from your investments WITHOUT actually selling them?!?!
